# Innovating Heavy Machinery to Tackle Global Megatrends

Welcome to our series on global megatrends, exploring how industries innovate to meet a changing world. In our last chapter, we could discuss [megatrends in the chemical industry](https://iprd.evalueserve.com/blog/cutting-edge-innovations-how-chemical-companies-are-tackling-global-megatrends/). In this installment, we will focus on the heavy machinery industry and its role in addressing significant trends such as urbanization, digitalization, sustainability, resource scarcity, demographic shifts, population growth, climate change, and global mobility. For a comprehensive overview of these megatrends, check out our introductory article: [Megatrends Shaping Our World | IP and R&D Evalueserve](https://iprd.evalueserve.com/blog/exploring-the-future-megatrends-shaping-our-world/).

In today's dynamic industrial landscape, heavy machinery companies are responsible for developing solutions that cater to market demands and drive future growth. Here's how they are tackling some of the most pressing global megatrends:

## Addressing Urbanization

As cities expand, the need for advanced construction equipment and infrastructure solutions grows. Heavy machinery companies like Caterpillar and Komatsu are responding by developing high-performance machinery that enhances urban infrastructure's durability and efficiency. Innovations such as [autonomous construction equipment and energy-efficient machines](https://www.caterpillar.com/en/news/caterpillarNews/2022/500-autonomous-trucks.html) are reducing the environmental impact of construction and paving the way for more sustainable cities.

**Challenges:**

**Durability of Infrastructure:** Creating machinery that enhances the longevity and resilience of urban infrastructure.

**Smart City Integration:** Developing equipment that integrates with IoT for intelligent infrastructure management.

**Efficient Material Use:** Designing machinery that maximizes material usage and minimizes waste.

**Technological Advances:**

**Autonomous Construction Equipment:** Machines that operate with minimal human intervention, improving safety and efficiency. Caterpillar's Autonomous Trucks set the standard.

**Energy-Efficient Machines:** Equipment that consumes less fuel and produces fewer emissions.

**IoT Sensors for Smart Buildings:** Integrated systems for real-time monitoring and management of building performance.

## Embracing Digitalization

Digital technologies are transforming the heavy machinery industry by enhancing operational efficiency and customer service. Companies like [Komatsu are integrating IoT, AI](https://www.sae.org/news/2017/12/komatsu-building-ai-into-construction-sites), and blockchain to optimize supply chains, improve machinery performance, and provide real-time data to customers. These digital platforms enable predictive maintenance and process control, ensuring consistent quality and reduced downtime.

**Challenges:**

**Seamless Digital Integration:** Ensuring the effective adoption of digital tools to enhance operations.

**Data-Driven Insights:** Leveraging big data analytics for informed decision-making and trend prediction.

**Cybersecurity:** Protecting digital assets and customer information from cyber threats.

**Technological Advances:**

**IoT Platforms:** For real-time monitoring and process optimization. [Komatsu's SMART CONSTRUCTION](https://smartconstruction.com) is a leading example.

**Artificial Intelligence (AI):** Machine learning algorithms for predictive maintenance and process improvements. Notable companies include [Alice Technologies, Assignar, Buildots, Caterpillar, Komatsu, John Deere, Volvo CE, Skema, Spacemaker AI, TestFit, ConWize, and Buildxact](https://builtworlds.com/news/blueprints-budgeting-15-companies-integrating-ai-construction/). AI is fundamentally reshaping construction, driving efficiency and innovation.

**Blockchain:** Secure, transparent supply chain management and traceability.

## Commitment to Sustainability

Sustainability is at the forefront of many heavy machinery companies' agendas, driving the creation of eco-friendly products and processes. Companies like Volvo Construction Equipment lead with sustainable solutions that enhance energy efficiency, reduce carbon footprints, and promote circular economy principles. Innovations include electric-powered machinery, biodegradable lubricants, and recyclable components.

**Challenges:**

**Emission Reduction:** Implementing technologies to lower carbon footprints and achieve net-zero emissions, contributing to climate change mitigation, and adapting to a recycling-oriented business model.

**Lifecycle Sustainability:** Designing machinery focusing on durability, recyclability, and minimal environmental impact.

**Efficient Resource Management:** Using materials and energy efficiently to reduce environmental impact.

**Technological Advances:**

**Electric-Powered Machinery:** Sustainable alternatives to diesel-powered equipment. Volvo's Electric Excavators lead this innovation. Also, [Hyosung Heavy Industries](https://www.hyosungheavyindustries.com/en/sustainability/summary/initiative) actively participates in various sustainability and social responsibility initiatives, including TCFD, CDP, Energy Alliance, and H2Korea, to promote environmental and social sustainability.

**Biodegradable Lubricants:** Eco-friendly lubricants that minimize environmental harm.

**Recyclable Components:** Machinery designed for easy recycling and reduced environmental impact.

Many of these initiatives align with the United Nations' SDGs, particularly goals related to Climate Action ([SDG 13](https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal13)) and Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure ([SDG 9](https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal9)).

## Tackling Resource Scarcity

With natural resources becoming scarcer, using and recycling materials is crucial. Heavy machinery companies are developing technologies to extract valuable materials from waste and improve resource efficiency. Caterpillar's focus on remanufacturing extends the life of machinery parts, reducing the need for new raw materials and decreasing waste.

**Challenges:**

**Maximizing Material Efficiency:** Developing processes to optimize raw material usage and minimize waste.

**Conserving Water:** Reducing water consumption in machinery operations. [John Deere](https://www.deere.com/en/news/all-news/2023-business-impact-report/) emphasizes sustainable resource management through various innovative practices.

**Sustainable Sourcing:** Ensuring raw materials are sourced sustainably and ethically. For example, [JCB is enhancing supply chain resilience and sustainability](https://www.jcb.com/en-us/about/sustainable-solutions/our-supply-chain) by localizing supply, improving logistics, embedding carbon reduction goals in supplier contracts, and reducing packaging waste.

**Technological Advances:**

**Remanufacturing:** Processes that extend the lifespan of machinery parts. Caterpillar's Remanufacturing Program is a prime example.

**Water Treatment Systems:** Efficient water treatment and recycling technologies for industrial use.

**Closed-Loop Recycling:** Systems for recovering and reusing materials in manufacturing.

## Adapting to Demographic Shifts

Demographic changes influence market needs and labor force dynamics. Heavy machinery companies are tailoring their products to meet the diverse needs of different demographic groups, including aging populations. Customized solutions, user-friendly interfaces, and ergonomic designs are essential. Additionally, companies are investing in workforce development to attract and retain skilled talent.

**Challenges:**

**Product Accessibility:** Developing user-friendly machinery for diverse demographic groups.

**Skilled Workforce Development:** Training and retaining qualified workers amid changing labor market conditions.

**Inclusive Design:** Ensuring machinery is accessible and easy for all users.

**Technological Advances:**

**User-Friendly Interfaces:** Advanced interfaces designed for ease of use across all age groups. [John Deere's Easy-To-Use Controls](https://www.deere.co.uk/en/tractors/mid/6r-series/6r150/) exemplify this approach.

**E-Learning Platforms:** Online training and development tools to upskill the workforce.

**Ergonomic Designs:** Products designed to be user-friendly, particularly for older individuals.

## Climate Change Mitigation

Climate change mitigation is a priority for the heavy machinery industry, with companies developing products that improve energy efficiency and reduce greenhouse gas emissions. Advanced engines, renewable energy solutions, and carbon capture technologies are critical focus areas. For example, Komatsu is working on hybrid machinery that combines traditional engines with electric power to reduce emissions.

**Challenges:**

**Climate Resilience:** Developing machinery that enhances infrastructure resilience to extreme weather conditions.

**Renewable Energy Transition:** Investing in renewable energy sources and technologies for machinery operations.

**Technological Advances:**

**Hybrid Machinery:** Equipment combining traditional engines with electric power. [Komatsu's Hybrid Excavators](https://www.komatsu.eu/en/product-archive/hybrid-crawler-excavators/hb215lc-3-hybrid) are an example.

**Renewable Energy Solutions:** Equipment supporting renewable energy projects.

**Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS):** Technologies capturing and storing carbon dioxide emissions from industrial processes.

## Responding to Population Growth

The growing global population drives demand for construction machinery, water treatment solutions, and food production equipment. [Companies like Deere & Company (John Deere) are innovating agricultural machinery and developing automated and precision farming technologies to increase crop yields and efficiency](https://www.news-jd.com/john-deere-is-enhancing-its-precision-farming-technology/#google_vignette). Heavy machinery for water treatment is also essential for ensuring safe and sufficient water supplies.

**Challenges:**

**Efficient Food Production:** Innovating agricultural machinery to increase food production sustainably.

**Water Management:** Developing efficient water treatment and conservation technologies.

**Food Safety and Quality:** Creating solutions to enhance food safety and nutritional quality.

**Technological Advances:**

**Automated Farming Equipment:** Machinery that enhances farming efficiency and crop yields. John Deere's Precision Agriculture is at the forefront, as is AGCO Corporation, which is involved in precision agriculture and has technologies aimed at improving farm productivity and sustainability.

**Advanced Water Treatment:** Technologies like reverse osmosis and UV treatment for clean water supply.

**Food Production Machinery:** Innovations that support efficient and sustainable food production. [Bühler Group](https://www.buhlergroup.com/) is widely recognized as a leader in providing food processing and production machinery.

## Enhancing Global Mobility

The shift towards sustainable transportation drives innovation in the heavy machinery sector. Companies are developing lightweight materials, advanced engines, and high-performance components to enhance vehicle efficiency and safety. For example, Liebherr produces advanced engines and components for electric vehicles (EVs) to reduce weight and improve energy efficiency. Additionally, advancements in battery technology are crucial for the future of EVs. For more information about smart charging to enhance global mobility, visit our [blog post](https://iprd.evalueserve.com/blog/revving-up-the-future-how-smart-charging-drives-the-electric-vehicle-revolution/).

**Challenges:**

**Vehicle Efficiency:** Developing lightweight, efficient components for sustainable transportation.

**Electric Vehicle Support:** Investing in technologies to improve the performance and sustainability of electric vehicles.

**Transportation Infrastructure:** Creating solutions to support the expanding needs of global transportation networks.

**Technological Advances:**

**Lightweight Composite Materials:** Advanced composites that reduce vehicle weight and improve fuel efficiency.

**High-Performance Engines:** Next-generation engines offer higher efficiency and lower emissions.

**Battery Technology:** Innovations in battery chemistry and design for better performance and longevity. [Liebherr Group's involvement in developing advanced components for electric vehicles (EVs) includes battery technology](https://www.liebherr.com/en/int/products/components/system-expertise/electric-drive-systems-off-highway-applications/electric-drive-systems-off-highway-applications.html).

By addressing these challenges and leveraging advanced technologies, heavy machinery companies are meeting current market demands and paving the way for a more sustainable and resilient future. Their ongoing investment in research and development ensures they remain at the forefront of tackling the challenges and opportunities presented by global megatrends.

SUMMARY:
With the quest for manufacturing sustainability with respect to energy, process, material, and environment friendliness as well as the clamour for circular economy which emphasizes zero tolerance for waste, there is a need for a critical review of the life cycle of machine tool employed for machining operations and product development. From the articles reviewed 60% applied life cycle assessment (LCA) methodology to reduce energy consumption and enhance environmental sustainability, while 40% employed other assessment tools. The impact of the machine tools on the environment is considered as the most critical factor during the use phase of a Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), because machine tools induces energy during operation (Santos et al., 2011; Hu et al., 2012; Akbari et al., 2001; Zein et al., 2011; Zendoia et al., 2014; Vijayaraghavan and Dornfeld 2010; Azevedo et al., 2011; Azkarate et al., 2011; Zhou et al., 2016; Hwang et al., 2009; Geissdoerfer et al., 2017).

Review article

Review of life cycle models for enhancing machine tools sustainability: lessons, trends and future directions
=============================================================================================================================

Author links open overlay panel Ilesanmi Daniyan a, Khumbulani Mpofu a, Boitumelo Ramatsetse b, Munish Gupta c

Show more

[https://doi.org/10.1016/j.heliyon.2021.e06790](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.heliyon.2021.e06790 "Persistent link using digital object identifier")[Get rights and content](https://s100.copyright.com/AppDispatchServlet?publisherName=ELS&contentID=S2405844021008938&orderBeanReset=true)

Under a Creative Commons [license](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/)

Open access

Abstract
--------

The life cycle models are critical in the assessment of the performance of a product from the design phase to its end of life (EoL). With the quest for manufacturing sustainability with respect to energy, process, material, and environment friendliness as well as the clamour for circular economy which emphasizes zero tolerance for waste, there is a need for a critical review of the life cycle of machine tool employed for machining operations and product development. The objective of this study is to evaluate the efficient way of managing the machine tools throughout its lifecycle. Several studies have been conducted in analysing the life cycle of the machine tools and different strategies were employed for its design, manufacture, use, maintenance and recovery at the end of life. The common approach to ensure environmental sustainability was established when comparing the literature studied. From the articles reviewed 60% applied life cycle assessment (LCA) methodology to reduce energy consumption and enhance environmental sustainability, while 40% employed other assessment tools. In this study an [integrated life](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/integrated-life "Learn more about integrated life from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") cycle and cyber physical machine tool model is proposed.

*   [Previous article in issue](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021009117)
*   [Next article in issue](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S240584402100918X)

Keywords
--------

Circular economy

EoL

Life cycle model

LCA

Sustainability

1. Introduction
---------------

The 21st century is characterised by vast technological changes, with many manufacturing industries faced with the challenge of developing cost effective products and innovation which will not only meet customer needs but also address the environmental issues. The term “Life cycle” can be defined as a closed cycle process with several stages within the cycle ([Peña et al. 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib140); [Finnveden et al., 2009](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib64); [Guinee et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib73); [Guinée and Heijungs 2000](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib72); [Klöpffer, 1997](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib101); [Hauschild et al., 2018](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib76); [Hellweg and Canals, 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib80); [Pennington et al., 2004](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib141); [Reap et al., 2008](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib150); [Simonen, 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib166); [Horne et al., 2009](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib82)). The various stages are given a specific name to differentiate it from one another, these stages move flawlessly and frequently to the next stage with no pause in between the stages [Matthews et al. (2014)](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib121). On the other hand, life cycle model is the beginning of the key concept of systems engineering which comprises of series of stages that are guided by several management decisions which qualifies the system to be well established to progress to the next stage of the system's life cycle ([Peña et al., 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib140)). However, there is no single system that is a one size fits all, such that it so versatile that it can be applied in all environments, each system coins its own life cycle model based on the characteristics and the needs of the industry. Material recovery at their end of life will assist in preventing of waste generation which contributes to the negative environmental impact at the product's end of life. Machining refers to the removal of material from a work piece during manufacturing in order to impart in them the desired shape ([Cao et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib23)). Machine tool is a machine that is used to shape or construct a metal by cutting, boring, [grinding](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/grinding-machining "Learn more about grinding from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") or as per the intention of the user. They are normally heavy and consists of components, clamps, etc. that are required to conduct a certain work or job. Manufacturing companies mostly depends on these machines for products manufacturing ([Mayr et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib122); [Boothroyd, 1988](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib15); [Vijayaraghavan and Dornfeld 2010](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib179); [Koren, 1997](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib103); [Mori et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib128); [Uriarte et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib177); [de Lacalle and Mentxaka, 2008](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib42); [Neugebauer et al., 2007](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib132); [Youssef and El-Hofy, 2008](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib189)). The examples of machine tools include; lathe, drill press, milling machine, screw machine, etc as depicted in [Figure 1](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#fig1). The machines tools can further be classified into dedicated machine tools ([Landers et al., 2001](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib109); [Mayr et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib122); [Chanal et al., 2007](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib24); [Xu and Newman, 2006](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib187); [de Lacalle and Mentxaka 2008](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib42); [Koren, 2006](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib104); [Valdes et al., 1997](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib178); [Huang et al., 2010](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib84); [Bringmann et al., 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib18)), Flexible machine tools ([Browne et al., 1984](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib19); [Stecke, 1985](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib170); [Mori and Fujishima 2009](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib127);[Kostal &Velisek 2010](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib106)[ElMaraghy, 2005](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib56); [Patpatiya et al., 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib139); [Cook et al., 2020](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib29); [Oshel et al., 2020](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib137)) and Reconfigurable machine tools ([Ding et al., 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib51); [Xing et al., 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib186); [Kumar et al., 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib108); [Mahmoodjanloo et al., 2020a](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib119), [Mahmoodjanloo et al., 2020b](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib120); [Huang et al., 2020](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib85); [Ming et al., 2020](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib126); [Bruzzone et al., 2020](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib20); [Wang et al., 2020](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib181); [Wang et al., 2018](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib180); [Gadalla and Xue 2018](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib67); [Olabanji and Mpofu 2020](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib136); [Murena et al., 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib130); [Battaïa et al., 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib12)). The classification of machine tools is shown in [Figure 1](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#fig1). According to [Cao et al. (2012)](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib23), the machine tools are basically employed to create a pre-determined shape on work piece material and to provide diverse services in the industry necessary for the processing of the components parts.

![Image 11: Figure 1](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr1.jpg)
1.   [Download: Download high-res image (189KB)](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr1_lrg.jpg "Download high-res image (189KB)")
2.   [Download: Download full-size image](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr1.jpg "Download full-size image")

Figure 1. Classification of machine tools.

[Duflou et al. (2012)](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib55) highlights the machine tools as tools necessary for imparting the desired shape in a material in order to meet the geometrical and dimensional requirements of the final product on a specified structure. The impact of the machine tools on the environment is considered as the most critical factor during the use phase of a [Life Cycle Assessment](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/life-cycle-assessment "Learn more about Life Cycle Assessment from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") (LCA), because machine tools induces energy during operation ([Santos et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib155); [Hu et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib83); [Akbari et al., 2001](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib5); [Zein et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib190); [Zendoia et al., 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib191); [Vijayaraghavan and Dornfeld 2010](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib179); [Azevedo et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib9); [Azkarate et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib10); [Zhou et al., 2016](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib198); [Hwang et al., 2009](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib87); [Geissdoerfer et al., 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib70)). According to [Diaz et al. (2010)](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib48) activities in the manufacturing industry accounts for 19% of the world's greenhouse gas emissions. In the United States manufacturing industry is responsible for the total energy usage of 31%. Recently, the machining in manufacturing has been targeted to reduce energy. The need to create awareness with regards to environmental protection and the possible impact of the machine tool development throughout its lifecycle has necessitated the need for the assessment of its lifecycle. This will enhance better understanding about the impact of each stage of the machine tool development on environment. Besides, the LCA of machine tools will provide an insight into the possible ways by which the impacts can be minimized. [Figure 2](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#fig2) depicts a conceptual overview of the life cycle stages of a machine tool beginning with the material extraction right through to the waste management and the recovery process in a circular loop in order to promote the concept of circular economy.

![Image 12: Figure 2](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr2.jpg)
1.   [Download: Download high-res image (568KB)](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr2_lrg.jpg "Download high-res image (568KB)")
2.   [Download: Download full-size image](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr2.jpg "Download full-size image")

Figure 2. Conceptual view of the life cycle of a machine tool (Adapted from [Duflou, 2008](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib54)).

The LCA addresses the potential environmental impact resulting from a product development and throughout its life cycle ([ISO, 2006](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib88)). The life cycle starts from the design phase and runs through the manufacturing, usage and end of life. Hence, a product's life cycle represent the birth to the end of life, and re-birth, which creates a cycle ([Gao and Wang, 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib68)). It promotes proper design, use and reuse of the product to minimize negative environmental impact. The significance of LCA to this study is that it helps to determine the environmental impact of all the stages of machine tool from the design to the end of life. The stage with the most significant contribution to the environmental impacts will also be determined. The understanding of the environmental impacts will enhance the development of sustainable solutions to reduce the environmental impacts associated with the stages of the machine tool as part of present or future directions. Some of the sustainable solution may include: redesign, reconfiguration, as well as adoption of a suitable end of life options. The concept of circular economy is a proven tool for sustainable manufacturing. The concept has the capacity for improving environmental sustainability, material and energy efficiencies throughout the lifecycle of a product. Circular economy adopts end of life options such as recycling, reuse, refurbishment etc. to minimize waste and reclaim materials o products into useful service after the end of their useful life ([Jawahir and Bradley, 2016](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib90); [Murray et al., 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib131); [Wu et al., 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib185); [Geissdoerfer et al., 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib70); [Peña et al., 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib140)). [Figure 3](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#fig3), depicts a product at it’s to end of life (EoL) with the possible (EoL) options to reclaim it back into service. As shown in [Figure 3](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#fig3), with the EoL options, the product can be reclaimed wholly back into service, or remanufactured for the emergence of a new product. The product can be reused, remanufactured, recycled, refurbished, repaired or reconditioned depending on the condition after the inspection at the end of its life. The stages create a cycle which allow the materials or products to be used again, in order to prevent the generation and indiscriminate waste disposal. [Phuluwa et al. (2019)](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib145), proposed a demanufacturing model for reclaiming products back into useful service after their end of life.

![Image 13: Figure 3](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr3.jpg)
1.   [Download: Download high-res image (271KB)](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr3_lrg.jpg "Download high-res image (271KB)")
2.   [Download: Download full-size image](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S2405844021008938-gr3.jpg "Download full-size image")

Figure 3. Sustainable model for product recovery (Modified from [Phuluwa et al. 2019](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib145)).

The manufacturing industry which is applicable to machine tools has experienced an increase in production and competitive pressures from their processing, this is due to the globalization and individualization within the manufacturing industry. These pressures are fueled by the growth of the new generation which mainly focuses on information technology through manufacturing. In addition to the concept of circular economy earlier discussed, intelligent manufacturing is one of the most important elements of the new industrial revolution, which is the digital networking and intelligent development of the manufacturing industry.

Another one is the sustainable manufacturing. This is one of the most important process to be adhered to in this era of uncertain global economy. This manufacturing involves the development of products through processes that are environmentally friendly, energy and resources efficient. Sustainable manufacturing has been defined as development of products via processes with optimal energy and [natural resources conservation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/natural-resource-conservation "Learn more about natural resources conservation from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), and minimal negative environmental consequences for employees, communities, and consumers ([Ball et al., 2009](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib11); [Branker et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib16); [Loglisci et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib118); [Lee et al., 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib111); [Yi et al., 2015](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib188); [Gupta et al., 2016](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib74); [Li et al., 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib114)). The quest to develop smart products which will meet functional requirements using emerging materials within a short manufacturing cycle time has led to increase in energy requirement of the manufacturing processes with environmental impact and cost consequences ([Priarone, 2016](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib146)). Typical examples of such emerging materials are the nickel-based super alloys, for instance, the [Inconel 718](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/materials-science/inconel-718 "Learn more about Inconel 718 from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), Grade EA1N steel, Gamma and Beta titanium based alloys amongst others ([Koohestani et al., 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib102); [Fratila, 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib66); [Daniyan et al., 2020a](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib38)).

These materials boast of excellent mechanical properties such as high [strength to weight ratio](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/strength-to-weight-ratio "Learn more about strength to weight ratio from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), excellent fatigue and creep performance, good [corrosion resistance](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/materials-science/corrosion-resistance "Learn more about corrosion resistance from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") ability, coupled with their suitability for [high temperature applications](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/high-temperature-applications "Learn more about high temperature applications from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") which makes them suitable as an alternative to the steel based alloys ([Kara and Li, 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib94); [Mhamdi et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib125); [Pervaiz et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib144)).

They find suitable applications in the biomedical, railway, aerospace, automobile manufacturing industries most especially in areas where special properties are required to meet the service or functional requirement of a component or sub assembly ([Kara and Li, 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib94); [Mhamdi et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib125); [Pervaiz et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib144); [Koohestani et al., 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib102); [Fratila, 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib66); [Daniyan et al., 2020b](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib40)). The limitation being the [low thermal conductivity](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/low-thermal-conductivity "Learn more about low thermal conductivity from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages") which causes the material to retain heat rather than dissipating it, thus, making the manufacturing process less sustainable ([Rashid et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib149); [Shokrani et al., 2016](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib160); [Ayed et al., 2017](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib7)). In order to promote effective manufacturing, cost and environmental sustainability, different strategies are employed for the implementation of sustainable machining processes. These includes: process modelling, simulation and optimization, effective [lubrication](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/materials-science/lubrication "Learn more about lubrication from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"), redesign and reconfiguration of tool and the use of the [hybrid machining process](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/engineering/hybrid-machining-process "Learn more about hybrid machining process from ScienceDirect's AI-generated Topic Pages"). These are to promote cost effectiveness, energy, material and environmental sustainability with improved productivity ([Dhananachezian & Kumar, 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib45); [Lee et al., 2012](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib112); [Elshwain et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib57); [Strano et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib171); [Djavanroodi et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib52); [Courbon et al., 2013](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008938#bib31); [Rotella et al., 2014](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2405844021008

The Drivers of Sustainability in Industrial Machinery · Digitalization drives sustainability · Optimizing the design process · Software solutions.

FULL CONTENT:
## Main Navigation

# The Drivers of Sustainability in Industrial Machinery

![sustainability drives industrial machinery](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/06/sustainability-drives-industrial-machinery.jpg)

A sustainable factory is a must in today’s competitive marketplace. Companies want to know that their machines, process, and delivery are sustainable for the environment and the overall lifecycle and costs.

Four key issues are driving the need for sustainability in the industrial machinery industry:

These drivers specify the need for industrial machinery companies to change how they view sustainability as essential and imperative to their business strategy moving forward. As a side note, you can also see how the need for electrification is driving the prerequisite for efficiency because so many of these processes require energy density that is only available in a fossil fuel, which is not the same way in electricity. So, there’s the need to drive down the energy used in a factory to convert materials that require evaluation and optimization.

Remember that the end objective is not merely a reduction in emissions from energy consumption of fossil fuels but also energy cost, which is on the rise. Therefore, there is the motivation to optimize that cost.

### **Digitalization drives sustainability**

Siemens combines the real and the digital world. Digitalization drives sustainability. Therefore, sustainability is an outcome that requires lots of planning and many tools to make it happen. Digitalization provides the opportunity to look at the entire product lifecycle, taking data from many different phases and visualizing it. This process allows you to try various scenarios as you run through simulation tools. It provides an environment where you can try many different variables, pulling things together within the context of digitalization, thus providing the ability to exchange data.

When you can view everyone in your value chain and ecosystem, there is much success with sustainability coming from people collaborating, exchanging data, sharing information, working together, and defending standards across competitors, moving forward into the entire area.

In the past, digitalization was viewed as helping a company to become a digital enterprise so they could be more adaptable, agile, and flexible to improve their ability overall to deliver the product within the right time, quality, and cost.

However, leveraging digitalization for energy efficiency, decarbonization circularity, and resource efficiency is bringing it all together comprehensively for our customers. It’s an evolutionary process. Digitalization provides the tools to make it palatable and executable.

So, as the scenarios become more complicated with more and more outcomes, we also provide digital tools and enablement to make it manageable moving forward. Managing or building in a complex environment can become more challenging; therefore, digitalization offers the tools to make it easier.

It also allows you to view the whole value chain and the entities in that chain working together to move it forward and find pain points universally and holistically to work together to address them. It provides a safe, secure exchange of data that allows people to visualize it across the entire lifecycle of a product, the production line or production facility. There are significant data implications for the industry and how those feed both traditional and sustainable outcomes.

### **Optimizing the design process**

When optimizing the design process using digitalization tools, you’re looking at the cost of the part from a supplier, the carbon footprint of that part, and the carbon footprint that the supplier may have in delivering that part to your factory or location. All of these are factors that can be evaluated upfront via digitalization tools.

This process provides the ability to perform evaluation and analysis in the realization phase when planning a product’s design and the machinery that will manufacture it. This is the perfect time when you can decide about energy-efficient equipment and how long that product will last. We hear people talk of brownfield factories, but there are also brownfield designs *(existing designs that you are trying to make more environmentally friendly)*, which are more complex than starting fresh with the information you need to make them environmentally friendly from the beginning.

[Learn more](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/podcasts/category/digital-transformation/) on this topic from our Sustainability Podcast series with Erin Devola, *Head of Sustainability at Siemens Digital Industries Software*, and Dale Tutt, *VP of Industry Strategy at Siemens Digital Industries Software*,

### **Software solutions**

**Siemens Digital Industries Software** helps organizations of all sizes digitally transform using software, hardware and services from the Siemens Xcelerator business platform. Siemens’ software and the comprehensive digital twin enable companies to optimize their design, engineering and manufacturing processes to turn today’s ideas into the sustainable products of the future. From chips to entire systems, from product to process, across all industries. [Siemens Digital Industries Software](http://www.siemens.com/software) – Accelerating transformation.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Implementing robust recycling practices in machinery design ensures that components can be retrieved and reused, significantly reducing waste and conserving raw materials.
Resource conservation involves optimizing material use during production and operation, which minimizes environmental impact and ensures sustainability throughout the machinery lifecycle.
Adopting circular economy principles encourages the design of machinery that promotes reuse, refurbishment, and recycling, thereby minimizing waste and maximizing resource efficiency.
